chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Next thing that we are going to learn about are data types.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Whenever we talk about a data in python,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example, we have created different variables here

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which are storing different types of data.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, there is a type of data in python.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, our fundamental data types are these.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: The first one is integer.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Integer means that any whole numeric value can be stored in it.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: It can be a positive number, negative number or zero.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly, float stands for floating point.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: All the decimal values in floating point,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which have point something in it,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: all the values like this are in float type.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: For example,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's clear this.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: If I have these variables,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if we want to print the type of this age,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can simply write print.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Just like we have print function,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have another function which is called type.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We can print any type of variable or number from type.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: For example, if I want to print the type of age,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is going to be int.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Int means integer.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Now, what is this class printing here?

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We will study that in the coming chapters.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: But for now, we have to ignore it.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We have to focus on this int.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This int basically stands for integer.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly, if I want to print for pi,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because pi is a decimal value,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: float will be printed.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly, if we want to print the type of name,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for name, our type is going to be str.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Str stands for string.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Whenever we have a string,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have already discussed in python,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can create a string with single quote,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: double quote,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and we can also create it with triple quote.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Along with this, we also have boolean variables.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Boolean variables are those

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which store true or false values.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, in boolean variables,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: either true value or false value can be stored.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: And this value has T and F as capital.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Otherwise, that will not be a valid value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because python is a case sensitive language.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, let us suppose,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have to store the true or false answer of anything.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: For example,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if we want to store for any number,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: is that number a prime number or not?

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We must have studied prime numbers

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in 5th or 6th class in our childhood.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, let us suppose, it is a prime number.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: If we have a number,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: value of number, if we have 2,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we know that 2 is a prime number.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, we have stored this prime as true.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, when we print the value of this prime,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it is going to be type boolean.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly, if we print false,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like 6 is not a prime number,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we will get the same value,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which is boolean value.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, boolean variables are those

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which have true or false values.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Along with this, we also have one more additional type

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which we will not use much for now,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which is called none.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We assign none value variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: when we do not want to assign any other value.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, we assign none to any variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which we do not want to assign any value.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: For example, if we do not have any number,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we can simply assign none to this prime.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, when we print its type,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it will be none type.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So, in this way, these are the basic data types

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which we have to remember.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Although, all of these are very intuitive,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it won't be very difficult to learn them.

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: And these words like integer,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: string, float, boolean,

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: are those words which we will use

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: while writing python codes.

text: Next thing that we are going to learn about are data types. Whenever we talk about a data in python, for example, we have created different variables here which are storing different types of data. So, there is a type of data in python. So, our fundamental data types are these. The first one is integer. Integer means that any whole numeric value can be stored in it. It can be a positive number, negative number or zero. Similarly, float stands for floating point. All the decimal values in floating point, which have point something in it, like 3.14, 9.99, all the values like this are in float type. For example, let's clear this. If I have these variables, if we want to print the type of this age, we can simply write print. Just like we have print function, we have another function which is called type. We can print any type of variable or number from type. For example, if I want to print the type of age, this is going to be int. Int means integer. Now, what is this class printing here? We will study that in the coming chapters. But for now, we have to ignore it. We have to focus on this int. This int basically stands for integer. Similarly, if I want to print for pi, because pi is a decimal value, float will be printed. Similarly, if we want to print the type of name, for name, our type is going to be str. Str stands for string. Whenever we have a string, we have already discussed in python, we can create a string with single quote, double quote, and we can also create it with triple quote. Along with this, we also have boolean variables. Boolean variables are those which store true or false values. So, in boolean variables, either true value or false value can be stored. And this value has T and F as capital. Otherwise, that will not be a valid value because python is a case sensitive language. So, let us suppose, we have to store the true or false answer of anything. For example, if we want to store for any number, is that number a prime number or not? We must have studied prime numbers in 5th or 6th class in our childhood. So, let us suppose, it is a prime number. If we have a number, value of number, if we have 2, then we know that 2 is a prime number. So, we have stored this prime as true. So, when we print the value of this prime, it is going to be type boolean. Similarly, if we print false, like 6 is not a prime number, then we will get the same value, which is boolean value. So, boolean variables are those which have true or false values. Along with this, we also have one more additional type which we will not use much for now, which is called none. We assign none value variable when we do not want to assign any other value. So, we assign none to any variable which we do not want to assign any value. For example, if we do not have any number, then we can simply assign none to this prime. So, when we print its type, it will be none type. So, in this way, these are the basic data types which we have to remember. Although, all of these are very intuitive, it won't be very difficult to learn them. And these words like integer, string, float, boolean, are those words which we will use while writing python codes.
